name: 2022 ACR/EULAR Classification Criteria for ANCA-Associated Vasculitis (GPA, MPA, EGPA)
classification_rule: Three separate criteria sets, one per subtype. Each uses additive weighted scoring with subtype-specific cut-offs. Target population: patients diagnosed with small- or medium-vessel vasculitis.
target_population: Patient with diagnosis of small- or medium-vessel vasculitis

key_imaging_criteria.label: Pulmonary nodules, mass, or cavitation on chest imaging
Granuloma or giant cells on biopsy
Inflammation/consolidation/effusion of sinuses, mastoid, middle ear

key_criteria.label: Pulmonary fibrosis or interstitial lung disease

todo: Encode all three scoring engines with exact EULAR/ACR 2022 item weights and the Chapel Hill nomenclature mapping — Day 2.

references.title: 2022 American College of Rheumatology/European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology classification criteria for granulomatosis with polyangiitis
references.authors: Robson JC, Grayson PC, Ponte C, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Current GPA classification. Derived from the DCVAS (Diagnostic and Classification Criteria for Vasculitis) study of >6,000 cases. Replaces 1990 ACR criteria.

references.title: 2022 American College of Rheumatology/European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology classification criteria for microscopic polyangiitis
references.authors: Suppiah R, Robson JC, Grayson PC, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Current MPA classification. MPO-ANCA carries strongest single weight (+6). Distinguishes MPA from GPA primarily on ANCA specificity + absence of granuloma.

references.title: 2022 American College of Rheumatology/European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology classification criteria for eosinophilic granulomatosis with polyangiitis
references.authors: Grayson PC, Ponte C, Suppiah R, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Current EGPA classification. Eosinophilia ≥1×10^9/L is the highest-weighted positive item (+5). Distinguishes EGPA from asthma+eosinophilia mimics.

references.title: 2012 Revised International Chapel Hill Consensus Conference Nomenclature of Vasculitides
references.authors: Jennette JC, Falk RJ, Bacon PA, et al.
references.why_it_matters: The reference nomenclature for all systemic vasculitides. Defines the vessel-size taxonomy and AAV as a discrete group within small-vessel vasculitis.

references.title: ANCA-associated vasculitis
references.authors: Kitching AR, Anders H-J, Basu N, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Comprehensive Nature Primer covering genetics, pathogenesis, kidney/pulmonary involvement, contemporary treatment (rituximab/cyclophosphamide/avacopan).

references.title: Avacopan for the treatment of ANCA-associated vasculitis
references.authors: Jayne DRW, Merkel PA, Schall TJ, Bekker P; ADVOCATE Study Group
references.why_it_matters: ADVOCATE trial — C5a receptor antagonist avacopan non-inferior to prednisone taper for remission induction; superior for sustained remission. Steroid-sparing evidence that reshaped AAV treatment.

references.title: Rituximab versus cyclophosphamide for ANCA-associated vasculitis
references.authors: Stone JH, Merkel PA, Spiera R, et al.; RAVE-ITN Research Group
references.why_it_matters: RAVE trial — rituximab non-inferior to cyclophosphamide for remission induction; superior for relapsing disease. Established rituximab as first-line for severe AAV.
